Karger AG","issue":"2","license":[{"start":{"date-parts":[[2008,1,1]],"date-time":"2008-01-01T00:00:00Z","timestamp":1199145600000},"content-version":"vor","delay-in-days":0,"URL":"https:\/\/www.karger.com\/Services\/SiteLicenses"},{"start":{"date-parts":[[2008,1,1]],"date-time":"2008-01-01T00:00:00Z","timestamp":1199145600000},"content-version":"tdm","delay-in-days":0,"URL":"https:\/\/www.karger.com\/Services\/SiteLicenses"}],"content-domain":{"domain":[],"crossmark-restriction":false},"short-container-title":["Dermatology"],"published-print":{"date-parts":[[2008]]},"abstract":"<jats:p><i>Background:<\/i> In addition to clinical efficacy, patient-defined benefit assessment has become an important outcome parameter. Thus far, no such instrument has been developed for vitiligo. <i>Objectives:<\/i> Development and validation of a patient-defined benefit questionnaire in the therapy of vitiligo. <i>Methods:<\/i> Open questioning of 50 vitiligo patients generated 110 benefit items, which were converted into a 26-item questionnaire by a panel of dermatologists, psychologists and patients. This was used to record patient-defined needs prior to therapy and to evaluate patient benefit attained after therapy. A \u2018patient benefit index\u2019 (PBI) was formed on the basis of both. Validation was based on data of n = 1,023 vitiligo patients. <i>Results:<\/i> The questionnaires were feasible in clinical practice, Cronbach\u2019s \u03b1 (patient need questionnaire) was 0.94. The PBI showed convergent and discriminant validity with respect to quality of life and patient satisfaction. <i>Conclusion:<\/i> The PBI for vitiligo is a valid instrument, which is highly accepted in practice for recording patient-reported benefit.<\/jats:p>","DOI":"10.1159\/000128992","type":"journal-article","created":{"date-parts":[[2008,5,6]],"date-time":"2008-05-06T14:30:07Z","timestamp":1210084207000},"page":"101-106","source":"Crossref","is-referenced-by-count":26,"title":["Benefit
